source_title: Unlocking Personalization: A Deep Dive into Modern Recommendation Algorithms | Sarang Gupta

concepts.name: Recommendation Algorithms
concepts.definition: Algorithms designed to suggest relevant items or content to users based on their preferences, behavior, or interactions.

concepts.name: Collaborative Filtering
concepts.definition: A method that predicts a user's interests by collecting preferences or behavior from many users.

concepts.name: Content-Based Filtering
concepts.definition: A method that recommends items based on the similarity between the content of the items and the user's preferences.

steps.explanation: To build a collaborative filtering model, you need data on how users interact with items.
Normalize the interaction data to ensure consistent scaling across different users and items.
Decompose the user-item interaction matrix to discover latent features that explain observed preferences.

gotchas.context: When new users or items have no interaction data.
gotchas.solution: Use hybrid recommendation systems that combine collaborative filtering with content-based filtering.

gotchas.context: When the user-item interaction matrix is sparse, leading to poor recommendations.
gotchas.solution: Incorporate additional data sources or use techniques like matrix completion.
